channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. B
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review B
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Microwave-induced transitions between weak localization and weak antilocalization in graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Jorge Navarro-Giraldo, Vinicius T. Santana, Oleksii Laguta, Dominik Bloos, Anastasia Bauernfeind, Marc Scheffler, Valentyn Laguta, D. Kurt Gaskill, and Petr Neugebauer

Weak localization and antilocalization are characteristic signatures of coherent electronic transport in 2D systems. Here, employing contactless microwave magnetoconductivity experiments with frequencies much larger than the electron decoherence rate, the authors report transitions between weak localization and weak antilocalization in graphene induced by the microwave frequency. These transitions fall outside the predictions of established models, suggesting the existence of novel mechanisms affecting electron coherence at high microwave frequencies.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125408] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jorge Navarro-Giraldo, Vinicius T. Santana, Oleksii Laguta, Dominik Bloos, Anastasia Bauernfeind, Marc Scheffler, Valentyn Laguta, D. Kurt Gaskill, and Petr Neugebauer

Weak localization and antilocalization are characteristic signatures of coherent electronic transport in 2D systems. Here, employing contactless microwave magnetoconductivity experiments with frequencies much larger than the electron decoherence rate, the authors report transitions between weak localization and weak antilocalization in graphene induced by the microwave frequency. These transitions fall outside the predictions of established models, suggesting the existence of novel mechanisms affecting electron coherence at high microwave frequencies.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125408] Published Wed Mar 04, 2026
item.dc:title: Microwave-induced transitions between weak localization and weak antilocalization in graphene
item.dc:creator: Jorge Navarro-Giraldo, Vinicius T. Santana, Oleksii Laguta, Dominik Bloos, Anastasia Bauernfeind, Marc Scheffler, Valentyn Laguta, D. Kurt Gaskill, and Petr Neugebauer

item.title: Frequency comb in twisted magnonic crystals
item.description:
Author(s): Minghao Li, Zhejunyu Jin, Zhaozhuo Zeng, and Peng Yan

While twisted magnonic crystals (MCs) have recently gained attention for their intriguing linear phenomena, such as magnon flat bands, their nonlinear dynamics—particularly the generation of magnonic frequency combs (MFCs)—have remained largely unexplored. In this work, we demonstrate the creation o…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104404] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Minghao Li, Zhejunyu Jin, Zhaozhuo Zeng, and Peng Yan

While twisted magnonic crystals (MCs) have recently gained attention for their intriguing linear phenomena, such as magnon flat bands, their nonlinear dynamics—particularly the generation of magnonic frequency combs (MFCs)—have remained largely unexplored. In this work, we demonstrate the creation o…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104404] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Frequency comb in twisted magnonic crystals
item.dc:creator: Minghao Li, Zhejunyu Jin, Zhaozhuo Zeng, and Peng Yan

item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Crystal symmetry paired spin-valley locking in the monolayered ${\mathrm{K}}_{2}{\mathrm{V}}_{2}{\mathrm{Se}}_{2}\mathrm{O}$ altermagnet
item.description:
Author(s): Hui Zeng, Weijie Zhang, Jun Zhao, and Dazhi Ding

The altermagnet (AM), characterized by zero net magnetization combined with momentum-dependent spin polarization, is regarded as a new type of collinear magnet and has attracted increasing interest since the fundamental concept of altermagnetism was established. Although many AM candidates have been…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104405] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hui Zeng, Weijie Zhang, Jun Zhao, and Dazhi Ding

The altermagnet (AM), characterized by zero net magnetization combined with momentum-dependent spin polarization, is regarded as a new type of collinear magnet and has attracted increasing interest since the fundamental concept of altermagnetism was established. Although many AM candidates have been…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104405] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026

item.title: Bipartite fluctuations and charge fractionalization in quantum wires
item.description:
Author(s): Magali Korolev and Karyn Le Hur

We introduce a quantum information method for measuring fractional charges in ballistic quantum wires generalizing bipartite fluctuations to the chiral quasiparticles in Luttinger liquids, i.e., analyzing and summing charge and current fluctuations in a region of the wire. Bipartite fluctuations at …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115105] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Magali Korolev and Karyn Le Hur

We introduce a quantum information method for measuring fractional charges in ballistic quantum wires generalizing bipartite fluctuations to the chiral quasiparticles in Luttinger liquids, i.e., analyzing and summing charge and current fluctuations in a region of the wire. Bipartite fluctuations at …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115105] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Bipartite fluctuations and charge fractionalization in quantum wires

item.title: Charge puddles complicate nonlinear transport in large Berry phase materials
item.description:
Author(s): Arya Thenapparambil, Kajetan M. Fijalkowski, Mohamed AbdelGhany, Hartmut Buhmann, Charles Gould, and Laurens W. Molenkamp

Nonlinear transport in narrow band-gap materials has recently attracted significant attention, being commonly recognized as a useful tool to study a variety of complex physical effects related to Berry phase. Here we demonstrate that a mundane, commonly overlooked mechanism stemming from tunneling b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125108] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:

Nonlinear transport in narrow band-gap materials has recently attracted significant attention, being commonly recognized as a useful tool to study a variety of complex physical effects related to Berry phase. Here we demonstrate that a mundane, commonly overlooked mechanism stemming from tunneling b…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125108] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026

item.title: Anomalous Hall effect in rhombohedral graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Vera Mikheeva, Daniele Guerci, Daniel Kaplan, and Elio J. König

Motivated by recent experiments on rhombohedral stacked multilayer graphene and the observation of the anomalous Hall effect in a spontaneous spin-valley-polarized quarter-metal state, we calculate the anomalous Hall conductivity for this system in the presence of two types of impurities: weak and d…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125407] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Vera Mikheeva, Daniele Guerci, Daniel Kaplan, and Elio J. König

Motivated by recent experiments on rhombohedral stacked multilayer graphene and the observation of the anomalous Hall effect in a spontaneous spin-valley-polarized quarter-metal state, we calculate the anomalous Hall conductivity for this system in the presence of two types of impurities: weak and d…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125407] Published Tue Mar 03, 2026
item.dc:title: Anomalous Hall effect in rhombohedral graphene

item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Diamagnetic Meissner response of odd-frequency superconducting pairing from quantum geometry
item.description:
Author(s): Ankita Bhattacharya and Annica M. Black-Schaffer

Odd-frequency superconductivity is ubiquitous in multiband systems but is commonly believed to yield a paramagnetic Meissner response and thus be unstable. The authors show here that this paradigm breaks down once quantum geometry is considered. For general two-band superconductors, the authors establish that the geometric interband contribution to the Meissner response from odd-frequency pairing is in fact diamagnetic, thus stabilizing odd-frequency superconductivity. In flat-band systems this geometric contribution can even dominate the Meissner response, which identifies flat-band materials as promising platforms for robust odd-frequency superconductivity.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094501] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ankita Bhattacharya and Annica M. Black-Schaffer

Odd-frequency superconductivity is ubiquitous in multiband systems but is commonly believed to yield a paramagnetic Meissner response and thus be unstable. The authors show here that this paradigm breaks down once quantum geometry is considered. For general two-band superconductors, the authors establish that the geometric interband contribution to the Meissner response from odd-frequency pairing is in fact diamagnetic, thus stabilizing odd-frequency superconductivity. In flat-band systems this geometric contribution can even dominate the Meissner response, which identifies flat-band materials as promising platforms for robust odd-frequency superconductivity.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094501] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Diamagnetic Meissner response of odd-frequency superconducting pairing from quantum geometry
item.dc:creator: Ankita Bhattacharya and Annica M. Black-Schaffer
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Nematic enhancement of superconductivity in multilayer graphene via quantum geometry
item.description:
Author(s): Gal Shavit

Multilayer graphene materials have recently emerged as a fascinating versatile platform for correlated electron phenomena, hosting superconductivity, fractional quantum Hall states, and correlated insulating phases. A particularly striking experimental observation is the recurring correlation betwee…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094502] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026

A particularly striking experimental observation is the recurring correlation betwee…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094502] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026

item.title: Berry curvature of low-energy excitons in rhombohedral graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Henry Davenport, Frank Schindler, and Johannes Knolle

We investigate low-energy excitons in rhombohedral pentalayer graphene encapsulated by hexagonal boron nitride (hBN/R5G/hBN), focusing on the regime at the experimental twist angle $θ=0.{77}^{∘}$ and with an applied electric field. We introduce a new low-energy two-band model of rhombohedral graphen…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115102] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Henry Davenport, Frank Schindler, and Johannes Knolle

We investigate low-energy excitons in rhombohedral pentalayer graphene encapsulated by hexagonal boron nitride (hBN/R5G/hBN), focusing on the regime at the experimental twist angle θ=0.77∘ and with an applied electric field. We introduce a new low-energy two-band model of rhombohedral graphene that …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115102] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Berry curvature of low-energy excitons in rhombohedral graphene
item.dc:creator: Henry Davenport, Frank Schindler, and Johannes Knolle

item.title: Electric field induced spin and/or valley polarization in Weiss oscillations of monolayer $1{T}^{′}−{\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yang Li, Wen Zeng, and Rui Shen

Monolayer 1$T{}^{′}−{\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$ exhibits spin- and valley-dependent massive tilted Dirac cones with two velocity correction terms in low-energy effective Hamiltonian. We theoretically investigate the longitudinal diffusive magneto-conductivity of monolayer 1$T{}^{′}−{\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$ by us…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115301] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yang Li, Wen Zeng, and Rui Shen

Monolayer 1T′−MoS2 exhibits spin- and valley-dependent massive tilted Dirac cones with two velocity correction terms in low-energy effective Hamiltonian. We theoretically investigate the longitudinal diffusive magneto-conductivity of monolayer 1T′−MoS2 by using the linear response theory. It is show…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115301] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Electric field induced spin and/or valley polarization in Weiss oscillations of monolayer $1{T}^{′}−{\mathrm{MoS}}_{2}$

item.title: Quantifying humidity effects on commensurability-dependent friction in bilayer graphene: A generalized registry index model
item.description:
Author(s): Binyang Shao, Xiaoyan Ren, Kun Liu, Lili Zhang, Yigang Cao, Xingju Zhao, and Shunfang Li

Deciphering the atomistic mechanism governing humidity-dependent interfacial friction remains a fundamental challenge across physics, chemistry, medicine, and materials science. Here, we employ first-principles calculations to systematically investigate the frictional behaviors of bilayer graphene (…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115402] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:

Deciphering the atomistic mechanism governing humidity-dependent interfacial friction remains a fundamental challenge across physics, chemistry, medicine, and materials science. Here, we employ first-principles calculations to systematically investigate the frictional behaviors of bilayer graphene (…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115402] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026

item.title: Synergistic effects of twist angle and substrate coupling on thermal transport in twisted bilayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Yiyi Li, Weitao Wang, Weikuan Li, Jianlian Huang, Yanping Qiu, Zhirong Shi, Wei Zhang, Yajuan Cheng, and Shiyun Xiong

The Moiré superlattices in twisted two-dimensional materials give rise to extraordinary electronic and thermal properties. While twist-angle tuning has been extensively explored for electronic applications, its synergistic effects with substrate coupling on thermal transport remain less understood. …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125404] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yiyi Li, Weitao Wang, Weikuan Li, Jianlian Huang, Yanping Qiu, Zhirong Shi, Wei Zhang, Yajuan Cheng, and Shiyun Xiong

The Moiré superlattices in twisted two-dimensional materials give rise to extraordinary electronic and thermal properties. While twist-angle tuning has been extensively explored for electronic applications, its synergistic effects with substrate coupling on thermal transport remain less understood. …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125404] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026

item.title: Symmetry-driven transitions between flat bands and Dirac cones in bilayer kagome lattices
item.description:
Author(s): Taylan Gorkan, Dogukan Hazar Ozbey, Cem Sevik, Milorad V. Milošević, and Engin Durgun

Flat bands (FBs) and Dirac cones represent two distinctive features of topological electronic systems; however, a unified mechanism enabling transitions between them has remained elusive to date. Here, we demonstrate a symmetry-governed and tunable transition from flat bands to Dirac cones in AB-sta…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125405] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Taylan Gorkan, Dogukan Hazar Ozbey, Cem Sevik, Milorad V. Milošević, and Engin Durgun

Flat bands (FBs) and Dirac cones represent two distinctive features of topological electronic systems; however, a unified mechanism enabling transitions between them has remained elusive to date. Here, we demonstrate a symmetry-governed and tunable transition from flat bands to Dirac cones in AB-sta…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125405] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Symmetry-driven transitions between flat bands and Dirac cones in bilayer kagome lattices

item.title: Resonating valence bond pairing energy in graphene by quantum Monte Carlo
item.description:
Author(s): S. Azadi, A. Principi, T. D. Kühne, and M. S. Bahramy

Whether short-range electron pairing can be stabilized in graphene remains a central question because pristine graphene is a semimetal with vanishing density of states at the Dirac point. We show that geometry alone can enhance short-range pairing correlations in confined graphene. Using real-space …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121101] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): S. Azadi, A. Principi, T. D. Kühne, and M. S. Bahramy

Whether short-range electron pairing can be stabilized in graphene remains a central question because pristine graphene is a semimetal with vanishing density of states at the Dirac point. We show that geometry alone can enhance short-range pairing correlations in confined graphene. Using real-space …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L121101] Published Mon Mar 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Resonating valence bond pairing energy in graphene by quantum Monte Carlo

item.title: Statistical study of atomic, electronic, and mechanical properties of amorphous graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Jun Chai, Mingping He, Hangjia Shen, Pan Liu, Jia Zhang, and Yi-Yang Sun

Amorphous graphene (a-G), the disordered counterpart of crystalline graphene (c-G), holds promise for flexible electronics, catalysis, and energy storage, yet its atomic-scale structure and property origins remain elusive. Here, we introduce a helium potential-wall method within ab initio molecular …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054203] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jun Chai, Mingping He, Hangjia Shen, Pan Liu, Jia Zhang, and Yi-Yang Sun

Amorphous graphene (a-G), the disordered counterpart of crystalline graphene (c-G), holds promise for flexible electronics, catalysis, and energy storage, yet its atomic-scale structure and property origins remain elusive. Here, we introduce a helium potential-wall method within ab initio molecular …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 054203] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Statistical study of atomic, electronic, and mechanical properties of amorphous graphene

item.title: Disorder-induced spin-cluster magnetism in a doped kagome spin liquid candidate
item.description:
Author(s): Arnab Seth, Joseph C. Prestigiacomo, Aini Xu, Zhenyuan Zeng, Trevor D. Ford, B. S. Shivaram, Shiliang Li, Patrick A. Lee, and Itamar Kimchi

The search for new quantum spin liquid materials relies on systems with strong frustration such as spins on an ideal kagome lattice. However, lattice imperfections can have substantial effects which are as yet not well understood. In recent work, the two-dimensional kagome system ${\mathrm{YCu}}_{3}…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064441] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Arnab Seth, Joseph C. Prestigiacomo, Aini Xu, Zhenyuan Zeng, Trevor D. Ford, B. S. Shivaram, Shiliang Li, Patrick A. Lee, and Itamar Kimchi

The search for new quantum spin liquid materials relies on systems with strong frustration such as spins on an ideal kagome lattice. However, lattice imperfections can have substantial effects which are as yet not well understood. In recent work, the two-dimensional kagome system YCu3(OH)6[(ClxBr(1−…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 064441] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Disorder-induced spin-cluster magnetism in a doped kagome spin liquid candidate
item.dc:creator: Arnab Seth, Joseph C. Prestigiacomo, Aini Xu, Zhenyuan Zeng, Trevor D. Ford, B. S. Shivaram, Shiliang Li, Patrick A. Lee, and Itamar Kimchi

item.title: Spin-orbit coupled periodic Anderson model: Kondo-Dirac semimetal and orbital-selective antiferromagnetic semimetal
item.description:
Author(s): Sebastião dos Anjos Sousa-Júnior, Julián Faúndez, and Rubem Mondaini

We investigate the periodic Anderson model composed of an itinerant $c$ band and a strongly localized $f$ band, featuring on-site electron-electron interactions in the $f$ orbitals. The two bands interact via a hybridization term with spin-orbit coupling, which enables spin-flip processes. In the no…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085149] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sebastião dos Anjos Sousa-Júnior, Julián Faúndez, and Rubem Mondaini

We investigate the periodic Anderson model composed of an itinerant c band and a strongly localized f band, featuring on-site electron-electron interactions in the f orbitals. The two bands interact via a hybridization term with spin-orbit coupling, which enables spin-flip processes. In the noninter…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 085149] Published Fri Feb 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Spin-orbit coupled periodic Anderson model: Kondo-Dirac semimetal and orbital-selective antiferromagnetic semimetal
item.dc:creator: Sebastião dos Anjos Sousa-Júnior, Julián Faúndez, and Rubem Mondaini
